message:
feat(tui): Improve SimulationHubOverlay UX with state awareness

Enhance the simulation hub with better feedback and safety guards:

Running simulation awareness:
- Track activeSim from store to know if a simulation is already running
- Display "(running)" indicator next to currently active simulation
- Automatically close existing simulation before launching new one
- Prevents orphaned simulation state from accumulating

Phage requirement handling:
- Check requiresPhage flag on simulation metadata
- Gray out simulations that need a phage when none is loaded
- Display "[needs phage]" suffix for unavailable simulations
- Show red warning text in detail panel: "Load a phage to start..."
- Block Enter key from launching when requirement not met

Header improvements:
- Display current phage name in header: "Current phage: {name}"
- Shows "None" when no phage loaded
- Provides context for which genome simulations will use

Visual feedback:
- Unavailable simulations rendered in dim gray (#4b5563)
- Clear visual hierarchy: available (white) > selected (green) > unavailable (gray)
- Running simulation highlighted for visibility

These changes make the simulation hub more robust for users who might:
- Try to start a simulation without loading data first
- Launch multiple simulations expecting them to stack
- Wonder which phage they're currently working with

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
